briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware firm Synapse, raising urgent questions about consumer protection in embedded banking.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving a wave of unconventional credit card products that blur the lines between lending, rewards, and financial wellness tools.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking startup Credit Kudos signals the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and alternative credit decisioning.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape, with implications for incumbents and fintechs alike.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada formally flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a milestone that continues to shape the country's evolving consumer-directed finance landscape.

- **[Canada is getting a sovereign wealth fund. What are they and how might this one work?](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canada-sovereign-wealth-fund-explainer-9.7179217?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — CBC breaks down how Canada's first national sovereign wealth fund could work and what it means for long-term investment in strategic sectors.

- **[Carney announces creation of Canada's first national sovereign wealth fund](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/sovereign-wealth-fund-carney-major-projects-9.7178238?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Prime Minister Carney has officially announced Canada's first sovereign wealth fund, positioning it as a vehicle to finance major national projects and boost economic resilience.

- **[Q4 Launches AI-Native CRM, Transforming How Investor Relationships Are Managed](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-wire-news-releases-pmn/q4-launches-ai-native-crm-transforming-how-investor-relationships-are-managed)** — *Financial Post* — Toronto-based Q4 has launched an AI-powered CRM platform for investor relations officers, automating meeting logs and enabling conversational data insights.

- **[AI ethical concerns roughly as important to Canadians as economic growth, analysis shows](https://betakit.com/canada-ai-consultation-analysis/)** — *BetaKit* — A new analysis of Canada's AI public consultations finds that Canadians weigh ethical concerns around artificial intelligence almost as heavily as its economic benefits, with direct implications for how AI-driven fintech products may be regulated.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[The Canada-to-Caribbean startup pipeline is real](https://betakit.com/the-canada-to-caribbean-startup-pipeline-is-real/)** — *BetaKit* — This piece explores how Canadian startups are expanding into Caribbean markets, a trend relevant to Atlantic Canada founders eyeing international growth given the region's geographic and cultural proximity to the Caribbean.

- **[Posthaste: Golden years disappearing as Canadian seniors in the workforce hits all-time high](https://financialpost.com/news/canadian-seniors-workforce-record-high)** — *Financial Post* — A record number of Canadian seniors are remaining in the workforce out of financial necessity, underscoring the growing importance of financial inclusion and retirement-focused fintech solutions in underserved communities across Atlantic Canada.

- **[I had £20,000 stolen and had to fight a 13-month fraud reporting rule to get it back](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cpvxg1vzxzjo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — A consumer fraud case highlights gaps in bank fraud reporting rules and reimbursement timelines, a timely reminder for Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs to evaluate their own fraud resolution and financial protection policies.
